content.problemStatement:
You are given two arrays, AAA and BBB, each containing NNN integers. You are allowed to perform the following operation as many times as you like (possibly, zero):


Choose an index iii (1≤i≤N1 \leq i \leq N1≤i≤N), and swap AiA_iAi​ and BiB_iBi​.
Find the maximum possible value that the expression

(A1&A2&…&AN)+(B1&B2&…&BN)\left(A_1 \& A_2 \& \ldots \& A_N \right) + \left(B_1 \& B_2 \& \ldots \& B_N \right)(A1​&A2​&…&AN​)+(B1​&B2​&…&BN​)can take after performing the given operation several times.

Here, &\&& denotes the bitwise AND operation.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of three lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains one integer NNN, the length of the array.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum possible value of the sum of bitwise ANDs of the arrays, after the swap operations.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : Swap A3A_3A3​ and B3B_3B3​ to get the array A=[5,4,15]A = [5, 4, 15]A=[5,4,15] and array B=[6,2,2]B = [6, 2, 2]B=[6,2,2]. Here bitwise AND of AAA is 444 and that of BBB is 222, adding to 666.
Test Case 2 : No matter what you do, the bitwise AND of both arrays will always be 000.
